behavior_summary: U8P_STANSTON::release runs when the object is released (Event 9).

The handler records or marks state on the subject by calling the engine 'set info' with info code 0x020D and a value loaded via an indirect pointer, then calls 'process exclude' to remove the subject from normal processing.
It queries the subject's Item::getSurfaceWeight() and, if that weight equals zero, calls AccumulateDexterity(15) to grant 15 dexterity/training points.

Overall effect: upon U8P_STANSTON::release the object is flagged/excluded (cleanup/transition) and may grant dexterity if it is weightless on a surface — likely modelling a small/light object being released and rewarding a dexterity interaction.
notable_control_flow: Step 1: push info id 0x020D, push subject (arg_item_pointer), push value from an indirect 2-byte reference -> set info.
Step 2: immediately call process exclude to unregister/exclude the subject's process.
Step 3: call Item::getSurfaceWeight(subject) -> compare returned value to 0.
Step 4 (conditional): if surface weight == 0 then call AccumulateDexterity(0x0F) (grant 15).
open_questions: What semantic meaning does info code 0x020D encode for this object (state, ownership, quest tag, animation stage)?
What exactly is the value loaded via 'push indirect 02h bytes' that is passed to set info (pointer, numeric flag, reference to a process or actor)?
Which actor/process receives the AccumulateDexterity points (the releasing actor, the item owner, or a global player context) — calling convention is inferred but not explicit in the excerpt.

Under what gameplay situations is Event 9 fired for this specific object (manual drop, thrown, auto-U8P_STANSTON::release) and does 'leaveFastArea' interplay with the U8P_STANSTON::release behavior in other scripts?

main_function.name: U8P_STANSTON::release
main_function.purpose: On U8P_STANSTON::release, mark the subject with info id 0x020D, exclude its process from further handling, then check the subject's surface weight and award 15 dexterity if the weight is zero.
entry_conditions: Event 9 (U8P_STANSTON::release) — invoked when the object/actor is released

function_map.name: U8P_STANSTON::release
function_map.role: Handles the 'U8P_STANSTON::release' event for the STANSTON usecode object; orchestrates state marking, process exclusion, and conditional dexterity awarding.

function_map.name: U8P_STANSTON::set info
function_map.role: Engine opcode to attach metadata/state (used with info id 0x020D and a pushed value/reference).

function_map.name: U8P_STANSTON::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine opcode to remove/exclude a process/object from further AI/processing.

function_map.name: Item::getSurfaceWeight
function_map.role: Query the item's surface weight/property; returns a word compared to zero.

function_map.name: U8P_STANSTON::AccumulateDexterity
function_map.role: Award dexterity/training points (called here with constant 15).

external_calls.purpose: Attach an info/state key (0x020D) and an associated value/reference to the subject; marks the object for some state change or bookkeeping.
Exclude/unregister the subject's process from further engine processing (cleanup or state transition).
Obtain the object's surface weight to decide whether to grant dexterity reward.
Grant 15 dexterity/training points when the object's surface weight is zero.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_STANSTON::release runs when the object is released (Event 9).
